title: Bridge Protocol (add-on <-> engine)
description: Messages of the bridge between the FreeCAD add-on and the AI engine. Transport: local TCP socket on 127.0.0.1 (ephemeral port, token handshake). Message format: JSON-RPC 2.0. Bidirectional communication: the engine can call methods on the add-on (e.g. run command, give perception) and vice versa (e.g. notify a user event).

handshake.description: At start-up, the engine presents a shared token (passed by the add-on when the process starts). The add-on rejects connections without a valid token.

methods._comment: Catalog of RPC methods for v0.1. ENGINE->ADDON are called by the engine towards FreeCAD; ADDON->ENGINE are called by the add-on towards the engine.

command.execute.description: Run a structured-vocabulary command inside an undoable transaction.

python.execute.description: Run free Python on FreeCAD. REQUIRES transparency: the add-on shows the code and an unambiguous banner before/during execution (principle 5).

params.reason: string (why the vocabulary wasn't enough — shown to the user)

perception.overview.description: Cheap overview of the active document.

perception.detail.description: Close-up look at an object/face chosen by the agent (geometric RAG).

params.target: string (object id, or reference to a sub-element)

transaction.rollback.description: Undo the last transaction (safety net, principle 6).

ui.confirm.description: Ask for human confirmation ONLY for irreversible actions (e.g. overwriting a file).

ui.highlight.description: Highlight an element on screen for disambiguation ('do you mean this one?').

user.prompt.description: The user sends a natural-language request. This is the real AI agent's channel (Phase 2+): the engine interprets the text and produces one or more commands. In Phase 1 (no AI) it is NOT implemented (the engine replies with a polite refusal).

params.selection: array (ids of elements selected in FreeCAD, optional)
params.ai_timeout: number (optional, seconds; per-call local-model timeout chosen in the panel. 0 or absent = UNLIMITED (default); a positive value caps each model call and overrides FREECAD_AGENT_OLLAMA_TIMEOUT)

result.cancelled: boolean (optional, true if stopped via user.cancel)

command.request.description: The user requests the execution of an ALREADY-structured command (chosen from the panel, not natural language). The engine VALIDATES it against commands.schema.json and, if valid, forwards it to the add-on via 'command.execute' (exercising both directions of the bridge). In Phase 1 it is the main channel of the 'fake brain'; it stays useful with the AI too, as an 'expert mode' that bypasses language interpretation. The engine may emit 'agent.status' notifications during processing.

user.cancel.description: The user interrupts the running task.

agent.status.description: Stream of agent status towards the UI (progress, plan step, self-correction outcome).
